---
title: "AutoParam System"
format: html
---

The **AutoParam** system provides a mechanism to automatically generate Qt-based configuration forms from C++ structs. It leverages compile-time reflection (via `reflect-cpp`) to create a runtime schema, which is then used to build the UI dynamically.

## Source Location

`AutoParamWidget` lives in its own static library at `src/WhiskerToolbox/AutoParamWidget/`.
It depends only on `Qt6::Widgets` and `ParameterSchema` â€” it has no dependency on
TransformsV2 or any other domain library, making it reusable by any widget that needs
schema-driven parameter forms (e.g., TransformsV2_Widget, TriageSession_Widget).

The CMake target is `AutoParamWidget`. Link it directly when you need the form widget:

```cmake
target_link_libraries(MyWidget PUBLIC AutoParamWidget)
```

### Include Path

```cpp
#include "AutoParamWidget/AutoParamWidget.hpp"
```

## Core Components

### 1. Parameter Structs

The foundation of the system is a simple C++ struct that defines your parameters.

``` cpp
struct MyTransformParams {
    float threshold = 0.5f;
    int iterations = 10;
    std::string mode = "Fast";
    bool enable_logging = true;
};
```

### 2. ParameterSchema

At runtime, we need a description of this struct to build the UI. `ParameterSchema` is a data structure that holds this information:

- **Fields**: Name, type, default value
- **Constraints**: Min/max values for numbers
- **UI Hints**: Tooltips, display names, grouping

This schema is extracted automatically using `reflect-cpp`.

### 3. AutoParamWidget

The `AutoParamWidget` is a generic Qt widget that takes a `ParameterSchema` and builds a form:

- `float` / `double` â†’ `QDoubleSpinBox`
- `int` â†’ `QSpinBox`
- `bool` â†’ `QCheckBox`
- `std::string` â†’ `QLineEdit` or `QComboBox` (if `allowed_values` or `dynamic_combo` is set)
- `enum class` â†’ `QComboBox` (enumerator names auto-populated by `extractParameterSchema`)
- `std::vector<T>` â†’ multi-select `QListWidget` or CSV `QLineEdit` (see [ParameterSchema](../ParameterSchema/index.qmd))
- `rfl::TaggedUnion` â†’ discriminator `QComboBox` + `QStackedWidget` sub-form per alternative
- `std::optional<T>` â†’ checkbox-gated widget

## Enum Class Fields

`enum class` fields (including `std::optional<enum class>`) require no
`ParameterUIHints` specialization. `extractParameterSchema` sets
`type_name = "enum"` and populates `allowed_values` with enumerator names;
`AutoParamWidget` renders them as combo boxes and serializes to/from JSON
strings (matching reflect-cpp).

```cpp
enum class BoundaryMode { clamp, bounce, wrap };

struct MyParams {
    BoundaryMode boundary_mode = BoundaryMode::clamp;
    std::optional<BoundaryMode> alt_mode;
};
```

Place enums in a **named namespace** â€” anonymous namespaces break reflect-cpp's
compile-time name extraction. See [ParameterSchema Â§ Enum Class](../ParameterSchema/index.qmd#enum-class-auto-detection)
for limitations.

## Tagged Variant Fields

`rfl::TaggedUnion` fields are rendered as a discriminator combo box above a
`QStackedWidget`. Each page is a sub-form built from that alternative's
recursively extracted `ParameterSchema`. `toJson()` / `fromJson()` read and
write the nested JSON object (discriminator field plus active alternative's
fields).

```cpp
struct LinearParams { float velocity_x = 1.0f; };
struct SinusoidalParams { float amplitude = 0.0f; };

using MotionVariant = rfl::TaggedUnion<"model", LinearParams, SinusoidalParams>;

struct GeneratorParams {
    MotionVariant motion = LinearParams{};  // default initializer required
};
```

`rfl::TaggedUnion` has no default constructor â€” the variant field must have a
default member initializer. See [ParameterSchema Â§ Tagged Variant](../ParameterSchema/index.qmd#tagged-variant-discriminated-union-auto-detection)
for schema details and JSON shape.

## Customization

You can customize the generated UI without changing the `AutoParamWidget` code by using **ParameterUIHints**.

### ParameterUIHints

Specialize the `ParameterUIHints` template for your struct to provide additional metadata.

``` cpp
template<>
struct ParameterUIHints<MyTransformParams> {
    static void annotate(ParameterSchema& schema) {
        // Add a tooltip
        if (auto* f = schema.field("threshold")) {
            f->tooltip = "Signal detection threshold (0.0 to 1.0)";
            f->min_value = 0.0;
            f->max_value = 1.0;
        }

        // Define allowed values for a string (creates a ComboBox)
        if (auto* f = schema.field("mode")) {
            f->allowed_values = {"Fast", "Accurate", "Debug"};
        }
    }
};
```

### Custom Widgets

If the standard widgets aren't enough, you can register a completely custom widget definition for your parameter type using the `ParamWidgetRegistry`.

``` cpp
ParamWidgetRegistry::instance().registerCustomWidget<MyComplexParams>(
    [](QWidget* parent) { return new MyCustomEditor(parent); }
);
```

If a custom widget is registered, the `AutoParamWidget` is bypassed entirely for that type.

## Runtime Field Updates

AutoParamWidget supports fields whose combo items or variant alternatives are
not fully known at schema-extraction time but are populated or filtered at
runtime (e.g., data keys from `DataManager`, or encoder types valid for the
current model).

### Dynamic Combo Fields

Set `dynamic_combo = true` on a `ParameterFieldDescriptor` to create a `QComboBox` even when `allowed_values` is empty. Optionally set `include_none_sentinel = true` to prepend a `"(None)"` entry that maps to an empty string in JSON.

```cpp
template<>
struct ParameterUIHints<MySlotParams> {
    static void annotate(ParameterSchema& schema) {
        if (auto* f = schema.field("source")) {
            f->dynamic_combo = true;
            f->include_none_sentinel = true;
        }
    }
};
```

### `updateAllowedValues()`

Dynamically repopulates a string/enum combo at runtime:

```cpp
auto keys = getKeysFromDataManager();
widget->updateAllowedValues("source", keys);
```

- Preserves the current selection if it still exists in the new values.
- Falls back to the first item (or sentinel) if the previous selection is gone.
- Emits `parametersChanged` only if the selection actually changes.
- Works on both top-level fields and variant sub-row fields.

### `updateVariantAlternatives()`

Shows or hides variant alternatives by tag name:

```cpp
widget->updateVariantAlternatives("encoder", {"ImageEncoderParams", "MaskEncoderParams"});
```

- Filters the variant combo to show only the specified tags.
- If the current selection is hidden, switches to the first visible alternative.
- Preserves the combo â†’ stacked-widget index mapping via `Qt::UserRole` item data.
- Emits `parametersChanged` only if the active alternative changes.

## See also

- [Schema-Driven Forms](schema_driven_forms.qmd) â€” end-to-end walkthrough from
  reflect-cpp structs through TransformsV2 pipeline composition, with a
  PostEncoderWidget comparison
- [ParameterSchema Library](../ParameterSchema/index.qmd) â€” schema extraction API
- [Widget Development Guide Â§ ParameterSchema](../WidgetDevelopment/index.qmd#parameter-schema)
